picks.thesis: Bullish setup on FDX: Earnings Surprise (+25.5%) — 2026-02-28 — actual 5.25 vs est 4.18261; Guidance (+8.8%) — +8.8% FY EPS growth est — raised/in-line proxy; 100D SMA (+13.7%) — price above 100D SMA $362.27; EPS Growth YoY (+17.3%); Revenue Growth YoY (+8.3%); P/E vs Sector (22.0) — 72% of sector median — discount; Free Cash Flow TTM ($894M) — positive trailing-12mo FCF; Net Margin Growth (4.4%) — 4.1% → 4.4% net margin QoQ.

takeProfit.reason: No resistance far enough above to bank into — target $470.79 (+14.3%), the chain's 1σ expected move by expiry. That's where the option has gained real value; beyond it you're betting on an outlier.

cut.reason: Reduce/exit on a close below $378.81 — losing an ~8% stop (nearest support sits too far below to be a useful exit) breaks the bullish setup that drove the call (thesis invalidated). Cut before theta compounds the loss.

reasons.technical: round number $470.00 at $470.79 (+14.3%) — where prior advances have stalled and supply sits. RSI would likely be stretched (70+) into this level, so sell into strength rather than chase an extension.
reasons.fundamental: Roughly prices in the next 2-3 quarters of the growth the trade is paying for — beyond it you're betting on an outlier, not the thesis.
reasons.mechanical: Into a run like this, put/call usually compresses toward ~0.5 (peak greed) — it's 1.33 now: a contrarian cue to bank the move.
reasons.narrative: Sell into strength rather than wait for the catalyst to fully play out.

levels.prose: Into round number $470.00, where the move is largely priced in — sell into strength.
levels.proseAi: Sell into strength at the round number where prior advances stalled.

reasons.technical: round number $450.00 at $450.00 (+9.3%) — the first real overhead. Watch whether it breaks on volume or rejects.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $470.79 (+14.3%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Take profits if the first overhead resistance breaks on significant volume.

reasons.technical: round number $440.00 at $440.00 (+6.9%) — the first real overhead. Watch whether it breaks on volume or rejects.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $450.00 (+9.3%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Take profits if the first overhead resistance breaks on significant volume.

reasons.mechanical: Confirm a break with 1.3x+ relative volume and rising call volume — a low-volume poke is a fakeout, so take the trim.

levels.watchFor: Breaks on 1.3x+ volume → ride toward $440.00 (+6.9%); stalls or RSI diverges → take the trim.
levels.proseAi: Take profits if the first overhead resistance breaks on significant volume.

reasons.technical: round number $400.00 at $400.00 (-2.9%) — the first crack below spot. Losing it on volume warns the cut is next; reduce rather than hope.
reasons.mechanical: Watch for a put-volume spike or fresh put open interest here — smart money hedging ahead of the break.

levels.watchFor: Loses it on volume → the cut at $378.81 is next; tighten up.
levels.proseAi: Reduce position if support breaks on volume, signaling further downside.

reasons.technical: A daily close below $378.81 (-8.0%) breaks the structure driving the call — the chart says the move is over. Don't hold a long call under broken support.
reasons.fundamental: A guidance cut or a macro risk-off rotation is what usually drives price here — exit, don't average down.
reasons.mechanical: A surge in put buying / put-call pushing back above ~1.25 confirms the turn.
reasons.narrative: A negative catalyst or a sector-narrative flip removes the reason for the trade — exit on the headline.

levels.prose: A close below round number $380.00 breaks the structure the trade is built on.
levels.proseAi: Exit most of the position if structure breaks on a catalyst or sentiment shift.

triggers: Exit before earnings (2026-06-23): the post-report IV crush can erase a long premium even when the direction is right.
Time stop: don't carry into the final week before Jul 17 '26 — theta accelerates and the breakeven move gets harder each day.
Thesis check: if a higher-scoring setup appears or the original reason for the trade stops being true, rotate out regardless of price.
exitPlan.overviewAi: Scale out on strength, trim on conviction breaks, and cut losses if support fails.

strategy.name: Pullback to Confluence
strategy.blurb: Buy the pullback into a zone where support, a moving average and volume all line up — the highest-probability entry.

entryPlan.sizingRule: Enter 50% at the first signal (the first pullback to $383.81), then add 50% on confirmation it holds (a green close / RSI turning up). Never add below the $378.81 cut — that's where the thesis breaks.

tranches.label: 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence

reasons.technical: 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence at $383.81 (-6.8%) — the zone prior pullbacks have bounced from. Wait for price to come to it; a hold with RSI turning back up is the entry, not a falling knife.
reasons.fundamental: The fundamental edge behind the score (Earnings Surprise) doesn't change on a routine dip — weakness into this level is price, not a thesis break.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm with rising volume on the bounce and call flow / call OI building — a low-volume drift through $383.81 is the cue to wait for the next layer.
reasons.narrative: Enter while the catalyst behind the score is still in force — if the story flips before the fill, stand down.

tranches.prose: Your first add when price comes to the 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence.

tranches.label: 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence — confirmation
tranches.trigger: Add the rest on confirmation it holds — a green close back above $383.81 or RSI turning up

reasons.technical: 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence — confirmation at $383.81 (-6.8%) — add only on confirmation the zone holds (a clean bounce, RSI turning up); that validates the entry and completes the position.
reasons.fundamental: The fundamental edge behind the score (Earnings Surprise) doesn't change on a routine dip — weakness into this level is price, not a thesis break.
reasons.mechanical: Confirm with rising volume on the bounce and call flow / call OI building — a low-volume drift through $383.81 is the cue to wait for the next layer.
reasons.narrative: Enter while the catalyst behind the score is still in force — if the story flips before the fill, stand down.

tranches.prose: 20D SMA + 50D SMA confluence — confirmation — add as the move comes to you.

entryPlan.summary: Pullback to Confluence — scale in over 2 tranches.

picks.analysis: FDX scores +18 — a Call recommendation. The bullish read is led by Fundamentals (+9), reinforced by Technicals (+7). The biggest contributors are Earnings Surprise (+2, +25.5%), Guidance (+2, +8.8%), 100D SMA (+2, +13.7%). Cutting the other way: 52-Week High/Low (-1, 99% of range). Within Industrials we gave FDX a +18 and CAT a +16 — same direction, lower conviction — which is why we'd take FDX calls over CAT calls, mainly on Technicals (+7 vs +2) and Mechanicals (+2 vs +0). The suggested calls sit ~9.3% OTM with 46d to expiry, delta 0.30, mid $9.75 — well inside the 5-30% OTM / Δ 0.20-0.40 / ≤$35 premium / standard-monthly criteria.
